# @json-express/api-rest — AI Context

The REST API generator (part of `@json-express/boot`). Turns every collection into CRUD endpoints and mounts model-defined custom endpoints.

## Install — that is the whole integration
```
npm i @json-express/api-rest
```
Discovery is automatic. **Never import or register this in code.**

## Configuration (.env — case-insensitive)
| Key | Default | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `jex.api.rest.prefix` | (none) | Global path prefix, e.g. `/api/v1` |
| `jex.auth.secret` / `jex.auth.jwksUri` | (none) | Shared `auth.*` namespace — when set, generated routes are auth-gated (works with `middleware-auth`) |
| `jex.auth.exclude` | `[]` | Comma-separated path prefixes exempt from the auth gate |

## What it unlocks (all driven from `models/`)
- Generated per collection: `GET /x`, `GET /x/:id`, `POST /x`, `PATCH /x/:id`, `DELETE /x/:id`; plus `?_expand=`/`?_embed=` and search by query params.
- Model `access` blocks enforced per operation (`read: "admin"`, `update: "owner"`, per-field read/write rules; owner records are auto-stamped and 404-shielded).
- ⚠️ **Mass assignment**: CRUD writes accept every body field by default. Sensitive fields (`role`, `emailVerified`, `passwordHash`, …) MUST be locked with a field-level write rule — `access.fields.role = { create: false, update: false }` — or a client can set them (`POST /users {"role":"admin"}`). A field with no rule is writable. See the skill.
- Model `endpoints: {}` mounted with `(req, res, ctx)` handlers — `ctx` carries `db`, and `email`/`kvStore`/`queue` when those packages are installed.
- Model `validation` blocks enforced when `middleware-validation` is installed.
